annotations.label: Representation (via action)

anchor.snippet: A representation of a group

annotations.note: The action-style definition is `MulAction G V`, which is the standard Mathlib formulation of a group acting on a type.

anchors.value: {\displaystyle \Phi \colon G\times V\to V\quad {\text{or}}\quad \Phi \colon A\times V\to V}

annotations.label: Representation (via homomorphism)

anchor.snippet: The second way to define a representation focuses on the map

annotations.note: `Representation k G V` is defined as the monoid hom `G →* (V →ₗ[k] V)`, exactly the homomorphism viewpoint.

annotations.label: Representation space and dimension

anchor.snippet: The vector space V is called the representation space

annotations.note: The underlying module of an object of `Rep k G` is the projection `Rep.V`; its dimension is `Module.finrank k V` (used e.g. in `FDRep.char_one`).

annotations.label: Faithful representation

anchor.snippet: An effective or faithful representation

annotations.note: Mathlib has `FaithfulSMul` for actions, but there is no dedicated `Representation.IsFaithful` predicate spelling out injectivity of `ρ`.

annotations.label: Equivariant map

anchor.snippet: then an equivariant map from

annotations.note: `IntertwiningMap ρ σ` bundles a linear map commuting with the group action, i.e. an equivariant/`G`-linear map.

annotations.label: Isomorphism of representations

anchor.snippet: are isomorphic representations

annotations.note: `Representation.Equiv ρ σ` is a linear equivalence that is also an intertwining map, i.e. an isomorphism of representations.

annotations.label: Subrepresentation

anchor.snippet: is called a subrepresentation

annotations.note: `Subrepresentation ρ` is a `Submodule` of `V` stable under every `ρ g`.

annotations.label: Irreducible and reducible representation

anchor.snippet: the representation is said to be irreducible

annotations.note: `Representation.IsIrreducible ρ` is `IsSimpleOrder (Subrepresentation ρ)`, the standard formalization of irreducibility.

anchors.snippet: the representation is said to be irreducible

annotations.label: Schur's lemma

anchor.snippet: The definition of an irreducible representation implies Schur's lemma

annotations.note: Schur's lemma appears as `FDRep.finrank_hom_simple_simple` and `IsIrreducible.bijective_or_eq_zero` in `Mathlib.RepresentationTheory.Irreducible`.

anchors.snippet: The definition of an irreducible representation implies Schur's lemma

annotations.label: Direct sum of representations

anchor.snippet: the direct sum of V and W is a representation

annotations.note: `Representation.directSum` builds a representation on `⨁ i, V i` from a family of representations.

annotations.label: Decomposable and indecomposable representation

anchor.snippet: Otherwise, it is said to be indecomposable

annotations.note: No `Indecomposable` predicate for representations exists in `Mathlib/RepresentationTheory`; the only `Indecomposable` files concern categories and monoids.

annotations.label: Semisimple representation

anchor.snippet: such representations are said to be semisimple

annotations.note: `Representation.IsSemisimpleRepresentation ρ` is defined and shown equivalent to `IsSemisimpleModule k[G] ρ.asModule`.

annotations.label: Tensor product of representations

anchor.snippet: Then we can form a representation

annotations.note: `Representation.tprod ρV ρW` is the representation on `V ⊗[k] W` via `TensorProduct.map (ρV g) (ρW g)`.

annotations.label: Clebsch–Gordan decomposition for SU(2)

anchor.snippet: In the case of the representation theory of the group SU(2)

annotations.note: No Clebsch–Gordan decomposition or SU(2) representation theory appears anywhere under `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Maschke's theorem

anchor.snippet: This is a consequence of Maschke's theorem

annotations.note: Maschke's theorem is `MonoidAlgebra.Submodule.exists_isCompl` with the immediate instance `IsSemisimpleModule k[G] V` in `Mathlib.RepresentationTheory.Maschke`.

annotations.label: Character of a representation

anchor.snippet: the character of a representation

annotations.note: `FDRep.character V g = trace (ρ V g)` and `Representation.character` give the character of a finite-dimensional representation.

annotations.label: Modular representation

anchor.snippet: Modular representations of a finite group G are representations over a field

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Representation k G V` covers any field `k` including positive characteristic, but there is no dedicated `ModularRepresentation` notion or modular-representation-specific theory.

annotations.label: Unitary representation

anchor.snippet: A unitary representation of a group G is a linear representation

annotations.note: No `UnitaryRepresentation` (or analogous Hilbert-space representation) is defined in `Mathlib/RepresentationTheory`.

annotations.label: Lie group

anchor.snippet: A Lie group is a group that is also a smooth manifold

annotations.note: `LieGroup I n G` requires a group structure on a manifold with `C^n` multiplication and inversion.

annotations.label: Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: A Lie algebra over a field F is a vector space over F equipped with a skew-symmetric bilinear operation

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra R L` is the standard typeclass extending `LieRing L` with `Module R L` compatibility.

annotations.label: Lie superalgebra

anchor.snippet: Lie superalgebras are generalizations of Lie algebras

annotations.note: Grep for `LieSuper`/`SuperLie` under `Mathlib/` returns no results; Lie superalgebras are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear algebraic group

anchor.snippet: Linear algebraic groups (or more generally, affine group schemes ) are analogues

annotations.note: No `LinearAlgebraicGroup` or `AffineGroupScheme` declaration exists in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Automorphic form

anchor.snippet: Automorphic forms are a generalization of modular forms

annotations.note: Grep finds no `AutomorphicForm`/`automorphic` notion in Mathlib (modular forms exist, but automorphic forms do not).

annotations.label: Set-theoretic representation

anchor.snippet: A set-theoretic representation (also known as a group action or permutation representation )

annotations.note: A set-theoretic / permutation representation is exactly a `MulAction G X` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Representation of a group in a category

anchor.snippet: a representation of G in C is a functor from G to C

annotations.note: `Action V G` bundles an object of `V` with `ρ : G →* Aut(V)`, equivalent to the functor `SingleObj G ⥤ V` as documented in the file header.
